cases.title: Aven and Dively v. Ohio Valley Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: TE Don Aven and TE David Dively

precedent_refs: Landrum v. MS Valley, Case 95-11, M26GA
Bowen v. E. Carolina, Case 1990-8, M19GA
topics: fundamentals of the system of doctrine
cases.synopsis: Two ministers complained that Ohio Valley Presbytery erred in granting TE Hickey an exception to Larger Catechism 177 regarding admission of covenant children to the Lord's Supper. The SJC neither sustained nor denied the complaint, remanding the case to the Presbytery because the record was insufficient to evaluate the minister's particular expression of his paedocommunion view.

cases.title: Sanfacon v. Philadelphia Presbytery
precedent_refs: Case 2001-32 Christ Covenant v. Central Carolina Presbytery, M31GA
cases.synopsis: An inactive ruling elder's complaint to the Session was filed 78 days after the Session action complained against, exceeding the 60-day window of BCO 43-2. The SJC ruled the complaint administratively out of order and voided all subsequent actions.

cases.title: Gearhart v. Chicago Metro Presbytery
cases.synopsis: An unsigned complaint was filed on TE Gearhart's behalf by a third party without standing, and TE Gearhart did not contact the PCA Clerk until 77 days after the Presbytery action (47 days past the BCO 43-3 deadline). The SJC ruled the complaint administratively out of order because it was not timely filed and the complainant did not give unqualified ratification.

cases.title: Flesher and Weekly v. Metro Atlanta Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: RE Steve Flesher and RE Randy Weekly

cases.synopsis: The SJC found Complaint 2015-03 moot because the issues it raised were adjudicated in the related Appeal 2015-08 (Hardie v. Metro Atlanta Presbytery).

cases.title: Thompson v. South Florida Presbytery
precedent_refs: Case 2013-03: Marshall v. Pacific Presbytery, M43GA
cases.synopsis: John Thompson filed a BCO 43-1 complaint challenging his indictment while his judicial case was still in process before the Session. The SJC ruled the complaint judicially out of order because objections to a trial court's actions must be raised by the defendant during the trial or by appeal after final judgment, not by a separate complaint during the pendency of process.

cases.title: BCO 40-5 Application of John B. Thompson re: Granada Presbyterian Church

parties.respondent_or_court: Granada Presbyterian Church / South Florida Presbytery

cases.synopsis: Thompson's BCO 40-5 application alleging grossly unconstitutional proceedings by Granada Presbyterian Church was filed with the SJC before South Florida Presbytery had opportunity to consider it. The SJC dismissed the application as prematurely filed.

cases.title: Presbyterian Church in America vs. South Florida Presbytery (Citation)
topics: citation for failure to respond to exceptions
cases.synopsis: The SJC reported that South Florida Presbytery responded to the citation issued by the 43rd General Assembly. The SJC found most responses satisfactory, but found one response unsatisfactory (regarding charges against a TE that were not properly processed under BCO 32-2), and noted an additional exception of substance regarding improper divestiture under BCO 46-8.

cases.title: Thompson v. South Florida Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Thompson's complaint to the SJC was prematurely filed before South Florida Presbytery had considered it at its next stated meeting as required by BCO 43-2. The SJC ruled the complaint administratively out of order.

cases.title: Hardie v. Metro Atlanta Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE John Hardie appealed his conviction and indefinite suspension by Metro Atlanta Presbytery's judicial commission, raising seventeen specifications of error under six categories including procedural irregularities, improper evidence, refusal of indulgence, prejudice, and disproportionate censure. The SJC denied the appeal, finding no clear error in MAP's proceedings and upholding the indefinite suspension.

cases.title: Bumgarner v. Presbytery of the Mississippi Valley
cases.synopsis: Bumgarner, a ruling elder who had been deposed in 2012, filed a BCO 40-5 application in 2015 seeking reversal of the censure after having previously withdrawn his complaint and acknowledged it could not be resubmitted. The SJC dismissed the application as administratively out of order because it sought to re-file a withdrawn complaint and because BCO 40-3 prohibits reversal of judicial censures except by appeal or complaint.
